`SecretsApp.list_with_properties` (that is used for the `list` subcommand) requires app version 0.4.11 (firmware version 1.5.0).  Otherwise, the last byte of the credential label is interpreted as the property byte (that is actually not part of the response).

Bug report: handle exceptions that occur inside of jinja
## Issue

### Issue description
Given sql with invalid jinja like:
```
{{ config(
    materialized = "table",
    schema = 'custom_schema' -- render this in a custom schema
) }}
```

dbt will throw a very unhelpful error that looks like:
```

    macros=macros)

    return template.render(ctx)
  File "/usr/local/Cellar/dbt/0.10.2/libexec/lib/python3.7/site-packages/jinja2/asyncsupport.py", line 76, in render
    ...

Which file was modified to fix this bug?

Bug report: [DOC] Misleading doc in SlidingWindowForecastCV
As mentioned in the comment section of #201, there is a misleading statement in the [`SlidingWindowForecastCV`](https://github.com/tgsmith61591/pmdarima/blob/master/pmdarima/model_selection/_split.py#L200):

```
    step : int, optional (default=1)
        The size of step taken to increase the training sample size when not
        performing using a window.
```

This is an artifact of when both CV strategies were lumped into the same class. This should read:

```
    step : int, optional (default=1)
        The size of step taken between training folds.
```

Good issue for anyone looking to get involved.

Which file was modified to fix this bug?

Issue: [DOC] Misleading doc in SlidingWindowForecastCV
As mentioned in the comment section of #201, there is a misleading statement in the [`SlidingWindowForecastCV`](https://github.com/tgsmith61591/pmdarima/blob/master/pmdarima/model_selection/_split.py#L200):

```
    step : int, optional (default=1)
        The size of step taken to increase the training sample size when not
        performing using a window.
```

This is an artifact of when both CV strategies were lumped into the same class. This should read:

```
    step : int, optional (default=1)
        The size of step taken between training folds.
```

Good issue for anyone looking to get involved.

Identify the file that contains the fix for this issue.

Bug report: stager_area RSEs don't work with a non-deterministic source RSE
Motivation
----------

If the source RSE is non-deterministic then even if the deterministic flag is set to false on an associated staging_area the staged replicas don't set the path, making them unusable.

Modification
------------

Set the path when the RSE is non-deterministic.

Which file was modified to fix this bug?

Bug report: MSMonitor fails to calculate rule progress: division by zero
**Impact of the bug**
ReqMgr2MS - MSMonitor

**Describe the bug**
There is one specific workflow which we hit this exception:
```
2020-09-29 23:54:53,133:ERROR:MSMonitor: Unknown exception checking workflow amaltaro_TaskChain_Prod_Sept2020_Val_200929_232610_2012. Error: division by zero
```

apparently the locks_* parameters are all 0. It's likely related to the current state of the rule `state=INJECT`, because this is a very large container (500TB).

**How to reproduce it**
Make a container rule for a very large container (perhaps > 10k blocks)

**Expected behavior**
If we cannot calculate the rule progress, then we should set its progress to 0%.
There might be room for improvement on the error message as well.

**Additional context and error message**
Error from the service log:
```
2020-09-29 23:44:52,078:DEBUG:MSMonitor: Rule ID: 4a2f8d276dbc40a98f2ea2a3582f2076, DID: /Cosmics/Commissioning2015-v1/RAW#487c3a70-b47d-11e4-8732-02163e00d625, state: OK, grouping: D...

Which file was modified to fix this bug?

Bug report: Probably invalid version checking in container creation
Hi.
I've stumbled upon something i think is a not up-to-date code.

utils.py at line 794 in my distribution (1.6.0) states following:

```
if compare_version('1.10', version) >= 0:
    message = ('{0!r} parameter has no effect on create_container().'
               ' It has been moved to start()')
    if dns is not None:
        raise errors.InvalidVersion(message.format('dns'))
    if volumes_from is not None:
        raise errors.InvalidVersion(message.format('volumes_from'))
```

As far as i can read from docs, this is not true - both Dns and VolumesFrom are seen in HostConfig for API 1.21. Even if everything is okay with this code, there is still a problem i'm struggling to solve - some of the HostConfig options can be specified in `.container_create()`, but not on `.start()`, e.g. logging driver and options may be set only during creation, but if i need to use custom DNS, i have to specify it on `.start()` call, and this will override whole HostConfig, erasing my logging configura...

Which file was modified to fix this bug?

Bug report: _run_until_complete never turned off
if a user calls ASB run_until_complete and then ASB normal run the system will get very confused.
asb._run_until_complete is set to True

then _run_until_complete is used for many decisions
It is never turned off

Not sure it will ever happen but I did not find any safety code in place if it does.

Which file was modified to fix this bug?

Bug report: Use appropriate log level for exceptions from event listeners
From @SebastiaanZ:

> Finally, `discord.py` currently "hides" errors/tracebacks that happen in event listeners as we only have a custom error handler for commands. This isn't too bad locally, since `d.py` **prints** those exceptions to stderr, but it obviously means they'll never show up in Sentry, as they are **not actually logged** with the appropriate level.

Which file was modified to fix this bug?

Bug report: Unable to install package in container
I was troubleshooting an opened bug (https://github.com/pypa/pipenv/issues/1639) that I seem to have run into. In trying to reproduce it, it seems like i bumped into another issue.

This looks similar to the closed https://github.com/pypa/pipenv/issues/1441 issue, however, the command `virtualenv --python=python3 myenv` did not error out as anticipated in the comments.

##### Expected result

I expected to see a `Pipfile` and `Pipfile.lock` in the `/tmp/test1` directory.

##### Actual result

Saw the strack trace above.

##### Steps to replicate

```
 saviles  ~  1  docker run --rm -ti fedora:27 /bin/bash
[root@0958ee759ab3 /]# dnf install -qy which
install-info: No such file or directory for /usr/share/info/which.info.gz
[root@0958ee759ab3 /]# pip3 install --user pipenv
WARNING: Running pip install with root privileges is generally not a good idea. Try `pip3 install --user` instead.
Collecting pipenv
  Downloading pipenv-11.2.0.tar.gz (4.1MB)
    100% |███████...

Which file was modified to fix this bug?

Bug report: VICReg requires torch >= 1.10
Hi,

The torch.cov in the covariance_loss function requires torch >= 1.10.
Replacing:
>      _, D = emb.size()
>      cov_emb = torch.cov(emb.T)
>      cov_ref_emb = torch.cov(ref_emb.T)
to:
>      batch_size, D = emb.size()
>      cov_emb = (emb.T @ emb) / (batch_size - 1)
>      cov_ref_emb = (ref_emb.T @ ref_emb) / (batch_size - 1)
would make it compatible with older versions as well.

Another remark is that in the reference implementation, the variance loss is divided by 2: https://github.com/facebookresearch/vicreg/blob/5e7b38f4586384bbb0d9a035352fab1d8f03b3b4/main_vicreg.py#L207
Was it intentionally left out, or am I missing something here?

Which file was modified to fix this bug?

Bug report: Remove optparse default values in boilerplate.py
The default values for username, password and port passed to optparse in boilerplate.py essentially disables reading these values from ~/.cloginrc. Due to the default values the `options`-object generated by optparse will always have values set and thus will the statements `options.xxx or cfg.get('xxx')` always evaluate to `options.xxx`.

Which file was modified to fix this bug?

Bug report: packaging does not expect 'extra' in marker (need vendor upgrade)
### Description

Hi, I've been doing some hacks on pip recently, like taking extra requirements into account when `checking_install_conflicts`. 
I have rewritten the `method` of `pkg_resources.Distribution`, and every time I get the dependency, I will return the extra mim requirements.
Here are the sample code:

```python3
from contextlib import contextmanager
from pip._internal.commands import create_command


@contextmanager
def patch_pkg_resources_distribution() -> Generator:
    from pip._vendor.pkg_resources import Distribution, parse_requirements

    origin_requires = Distribution.requires

    def patched_requires(self, extras=()):
        deps = origin_requires(self, extras)
        if 'mim' in self.extras:
            mim_extra_requires = origin_requires(self, ('mim',))
            filter_invalid_marker(mim_extra_requires)
            deps += mim_extra_requires
        return deps

    Distribution.requires = patched_requires  # type: ignore
    yield...

Which file was modified to fix this bug?

Issue: packaging does not expect 'extra' in marker (need vendor upgrade)
### Description

Hi, I've been doing some hacks on pip recently, like taking extra requirements into account when `checking_install_conflicts`. 
I have rewritten the `method` of `pkg_resources.Distribution`, and every time I get the dependency, I will return the extra mim requirements.
Here are the sample code:

```python3
from contextlib import contextmanager
from pip._internal.commands import create_command


@contextmanager
def patch_pkg_resources_distribution() -> Generator:
    from pip._vendor.pkg_resources import Distribution, parse_requirements

    origin_requires = Distribution.requires

    def patched_requires(self, extras=()):
        deps = origin_requires(self, extras)
        if 'mim' in self.extras:
            mim_extra_requires = origin_requires(self, ('mim',))
            filter_invalid_marker(mim_extra_requires)
            deps += mim_extra_requires
        return deps

    Distribution.requires = patched_requires  # type: ignore
    yield...

Bug report: ExecPlugins are broken when using KubeConfigs loaded from dict
**What happened (please include outputs or screenshots)**:

We use https://github.com/kubernetes-client/python/blob/master/kubernetes/base/config/kube_config.py#L820 to load Kubeconfigs from memory in Python, these Kubeconfigs have a configuration to use an Exec plugin to get an auth token (specifically we are trying to auth with an EKS cluster.)

https://github.com/kubernetes-client/python-base/pull/275/files#diff-a994e720c2c925ca342b251f2d057ab3a730a1bdc166e5766249a30bdf170a47R73 this PR to python-base attempts to pass the cwd of the config file to the exec plugin, however the cwd of a config file loaded from a dict is empty string (https://github.com/kubernetes-client/python/blob/master/kubernetes/base/config/kube_config.py#L542). When empty string is passed to subprocess.Popen, subprocess.Popen errors with "ERROR:root:[Errno 2] No such file or directory: ''" and does not run the auth plugin. 

Commenting out https://github.com/kubernetes-client/python-base/pull/275/files#diff...

Which file was modified to fix this bug?

Bug report: AttributeError: Dataset has no self._logger set
**Describe the bug**
In the `__init__`  of `Dataset` references to `self._logger` is made through calls such as `self.warning(msg)`. However, these calls fail with `AttributeError` because the `super().__init__(name=__name__, class_name=self.__class__.__name__)` (which sets the `self._logger` via inheritence to `Logger`) comes **after** these messages are attempted to be sent to the logger.

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Run any training script with `string_selection = [0,1,2,3,4,5]` set for `SQLiteDataset`

    self.warning(
  File "/home/iwsatlas1/oersoe/github/graphnet/src/graphnet/utilities/logging.py", line 213, in warning
    return self._logger.warning(msg, *args, **kwargs)
  File "/opt/anaconda3/lib/python3.9/site-packages/torch/utils/data/dataset.py", line 83, i...

Which file was modified to fix this bug?

Bug report: API lag may lead to more than 2 available help channels
`on_message` uses a lock to ensure messages sent in available channels are processed sequentially. After it acquires the lock, it checks if the channel has already been moved out of available. If it has, then it aborts further processing. The problem is that this relies on Discord moving the channel in a reasonable amount of time. If it happens slowly, then the next message that acquires the lock will still think the channel can be claimed. 

This is because only the request to move the channel is awaited. It can be sure the request has been sent, but not that Discord has processed it and moved the channel. To do that, it'd have to wait for a guild channel update event. That's just one solution. Another idea I have is to check if the channel has a claimant associated with it, since that is already tracked for other purposes.

Which file was modified to fix this bug?

Bug report: Spurious "can't determine whether the indexer has a time zone" warnings
**Describe the bug**
When producing time-series plots in the viewer from user-selected geometries, the server outputs this warning repeatedly:

> UserWarning: We can't determine whether the time indexer has a time zone. We will therefore omit the check on whether the time indexer and the variable are incompatible in terms of timezone awareness. This may result in an error when an indexing operation is carried out. If such an error occurs after this warning, make sure that the time indexer has timezone information.

> FutureWarning: Indexing a timezone-naive DatetimeIndex with a timezone-aware datetime is deprecated and will raise KeyError in a future version.  Use a timezone-naive object instead.

```bash

Bug report: DataTable resize not happening on column resize and also resizing oddly
When updating a cell inside of a DataTable, if the width of the updated cell goes outside of the window bounds the DataTable container doesn't resize to allow scrolling so you can't see the updated content. Oddly enough, if you update the cell a second time the DataTable container does resize and the scrollbars are drawn. You can see the Resize method being called from the console on the second update but not the first.

And a weird side-effect of the resize (from a user view/interaction stand point), is the last row of the table which was previously viewable is "covered" by the horizontal scrollbar after the resize. I tried digging into the Widget `refresh()` method, but it seemed like somewhere I shouldn't be messing around without more knowledge of the API.

Which file was modified to fix this bug?

Bug report: Exceptions when task is pending is vague and unhandleable
**Describe the bug**
Using the funcx client's `client.get_result(<task_uuid>)` returns a naked `Exception` with a message in it when the task is pending. Due to this exception not being typed better, it is very difficult to programmatically handle this. 

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior, for e.g:
1. Run a slow function
2. Do `funcx_client.get_result(<task_uuid_of_slow_function>)`
3. Get the `Exception('waiting-for-ep')` exception
4. Be annoyed because a try block cannot differentiate this from an arbitrary exception

**Expected behavior**
The client should raise a clearly typed exception, say, `funcx.sdk.errors.TaskPending` or `TaskNotComplete`

Which file was modified to fix this bug?

Bug report: download multiple assets example bypasses rate limiting by using multiple sessions
**Expected behavior**
[download_multiple_assets.py example](https://github.com/planetlabs/planet-client-python/blob/main/examples/download_multiple_assets.py) should use Session in a way that enables rate-limiting (that, is, use only one Session). See [orders_create_and_download_multiple_orders.py](https://github.com/planetlabs/planet-client-python/blob/main/examples/orders_create_and_download_multiple_orders.py) for an example

**Actual behavior (describe the problem)**
[download_multiple_assets.py example](https://github.com/planetlabs/planet-client-python/blob/main/examples/download_multiple_assets.py) creates a Session for each download task, thus bypassing rate-limiting.

Which file was modified to fix this bug?

Issue: download multiple assets example bypasses rate limiting by using multiple sessions
**Expected behavior**
[download_multiple_assets.py example](https://github.com/planetlabs/planet-client-python/blob/main/examples/download_multiple_assets.py) should use Session in a way that enables rate-limiting (that, is, use only one Session). See [orders_create_and_download_multiple_orders.py](https://github.com/planetlabs/planet-client-python/blob/main/examples/orders_create_and_download_multiple_orders.py) for an example

**Actual behavior (describe the problem)**
[download_multiple_assets.py example](https://github.com/planetlabs/planet-client-python/blob/main/examples/download_multiple_assets.py) creates a Session for each download task, thus bypassing rate-limiting.

Bug report: Parallel execution ran into "patch does not apply"
I modified a bunch of repos using `pre-commit` in parallel and ran `git commit` at the same time, with unstaged changes. The pre-commit processes did `[WARNING] Unstaged files detected.`, stashed the changes in a pach, ran, and then tried to reapply the patches.

Some repos failed with:


```
[WARNING] Stashed changes conflicted with hook auto-fixes... Rolling back fixes...
An unexpected error has occurred: CalledProcessError: command: ('/usr/local/Cellar/git/2.31.1/libexec/git-core/git', '-c', 'core.autocrlf=false', 'apply', '--whitespace=nowarn', '/Users/chainz/.cache/pre-commit/patch1618586253')
return code: 1
expected return code: 0
stdout: (none)
stderr:
    error: patch failed: .github/workflows/main.yml:21
    error: .github/workflows/main.yml: patch does not apply

Check the log at /Users/chainz/.cache/pre-commit/pre-commit.log
```

It looks like this is due to use of the unix timestamp as the only differentiator in patch file paths, causing the parallely-crea...

Which file was modified to fix this bug?

Inplace: `self.data += tensor.data`
Not inplace: `self.data = self.data + tensor.data`

Bug report: core/rule.py reencodes meta over and over again
Motivation
----------

core/replica.py reencodes meta to JSON over and over again inside a loop on DIDs. Eventually this makes a string so big it causes an exception in oracle. It's not clear if this also corrupts the intended metadata

Modification
------------

Use a dedicated variable for the results of the JSON encoding

Which file was modified to fix this bug?

Bug report: [BUG]: Poisoning activation defence gives error while extracting outputs of activation layers
### Description of the bug

This could be because the Resnet50 version of tensorflow from https://github.com/keras-team/keras/blob/b80dd12da9c0bc3f569eca3455e77762cf2ee8ef/keras/applications/resnet.py#L76 is not declared as tf.keras.models.sequential whereas the get_activations in https://github.com/Trusted-AI/adversarial-robustness-toolbox/blob/main/art/estimators/classification/tensorflow.py requires the model to be tf.keras.models.Sequential.

`ValueError: Method get_activations is not supported for non-Sequential models.`

Is there any quick fix?

Bug report: Typo in raise from einsum
### Description

In this line:

https://github.com/cupy/cupy/blob/master/cupy/linalg/_einsum.py#L511

einsum() tries to raise an error:

```py
raise TypeError('Did not understand the following kwargs: %s'
                        % list(kwargs.keys))
```

but I believe it should be `list(kwargs.keys())` or simply `list(kwargs)`.

Bug report: `check-useless-excludes` meta hook doesn't seem to work well with broken symlinks
Repro steps
1. Add a submodule
2. Add a symlink to somewhere inside this submodule
3. Use `check-symlinks`
4. Deinit submodule (to simulate the case when people clone without `--recursive`)
4. Notice that it now complains about the symlink created on 2
5. Add an exclude entry with the path to that symlink
6. Use `check-useless-excludes`
7. Notice that it complains that the added exclude entry doesn't apply.

For a working example, check out https://github.com/pkoch/test-check-useless-excludes (without `--recursive`, or run `git submodule deinit --all`) and run `pre-commit run --all`.

Which file was modified to fix this bug?

Bug report: linear_transpose involving fft seemingly incorrect

I noticed that the fft module seems to implement the rules for transposition; the last few lines of https://jax.readthedocs.io/en/latest/_modules/jax/_src/lax/fft.html seem to pertain to it.

I am not familiar enough with the jax API at present to point out any bugs; but below is a simple test that demonstrates what I believe to be a bug. The build_matrix helper function explicitly constructs the coefficients of the linear function by feeding it all delta functions in sequence. If I linear_transpose my function, that should be identical to the matrix transpose of the built matrix. Yet it doesnt. It seems as if im getting my result in reverse order (plus another off-by-one index bug I think).

Seems to me like a bug in the implementation of the transpose rules for ffts; but again not qualified myself to spot it.

Bug report: Release candidate 1.20.4rc1 is unstable
Motivation
----------
There are issues with the release candidate:
- reaper2: messages fail to serialize
- judge: hangs on some requests
- REST list_replicas: TypeError, keys must be a string

Modification
------------
Reaper2 messages should be external representations.
Judge is hanging as the string type new internaltypes use with the backend db is different. This causes the indexes to be ignored and a query can need to scan the full table. This type needs to be changed from unicode.

Which file was modified to fix this bug?

Bug report: Recovering the mode of the model after running evaluator
When the evaluator is run, `ppe.handler.logic.train_validation_begin` changes the model to `eval` mode, but the `ppe.handler.logic.train_ validation_end`, which is executed after the evaluator is run, does not restore it.

Conventionally, it is specified in `train` mode via `ppe.handler.logic.train_epoch_begin`, but if the timing of the evaluator execution is at a non-integer epoch frequency, it is possible that a training that is not in `train` mode will be executed.

Therefore, the mode of the model is restored at `ppe.handler.logic.train_validation_end`.

https://github.com/pfnet/pytorch-pfn-extras/blob/bcb5f37eba7ffd48f052231c886df21ab759a0f8/pytorch_pfn_extras/handler/_logic.py#L330-L340

Which file was modified to fix this bug?

Bug report: print_filtered_stacktrace hides chained exceptions
```python
import sacred

try:
    raise Exception('e1') from Exception('e2')
except Exception as e:
    print('#### Message from print_filtered_stacktrace ####')
    sacred.utils.print_filtered_stacktrace()
    print('#### Original message ####')
    raise e
```
```
#### Message from print_filtered_stacktrace ####
Traceback (most recent calls WITHOUT Sacred internals):
  File "a.py", line 3, in <module>
    raise Exception('e1') from Exception('e2')
Exception: e1
#### Original message ####
Exception: e2

The above exception was the direct cause of the following exception:

Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "a.py", line 8, in <module>
    raise e
  File "a.py", line 3, in <module>
    raise Exception('e1') from Exception('e2')
Exception: e1
```
print_filtered_stacktrace is used at https://github.com/IDSIA/sacred/blob/master/sacred/experiment.py#L255, and it hides the traceback from chained exceptions.

print_filtered_stacktrace doesn't call  traceb...

Which file was modified to fix this bug?

Bug report: Mesos and single-machine treat default requirements differently
The following user script

```
import sys
from toil.job import Job

def hello( name ):
    return "Hello, %s!" % name

if __name__ == '__main__':

    job = Job.wrapFn( hello, "world" )
    result = Job.Runner.startToil( job, options )
    assert result == 'Hello, world!'
```

passes with single machine and hangs with Mesos.

```
python2.7 hello_world.py --batchSystem=mesos --mesosMaster=mesos-master:5050 aws:us-west-2:foo
python2.7 hello_world.py aws:us-west-2:foo
```

Bug report: Reaper 2.0 Should Reap Greedy RSE's Without Needing MinFreeSpace/MaxBeingDeletedFiles Set
Motivation
----------
The current version of the Reaper 2.0 will not reap an RSE marked as greedy that does not have MinFreeSpace and MaxBeingDeletedFiles set. MinFreeSpace should not matter for a greedy RSE, and MaxBeingDeletedFiles is not used by the Reaper 2.


Modification
------------
Move the check for greedy mode in __check_rse_usage() to before the acquisition of RSE limits.

Which file was modified to fix this bug?

Bug report: Old webhooks gives 502
Running this command,

    $ http POST https://readthedocs.org/github repository:='{"url": "", "ssh_url": ""}' ref=""

breaks the site and may produce tons of tasks being ran.

After running that command I get 502 and I tried it locally and I found that the problem is at the Project query since all the repo urls ends with `''` 

https://github.com/rtfd/readthedocs.org/blob/445683fc0c86b0de56779447a8eedf98024dba32/readthedocs/core/views/hooks.py#L92-L96

Finally, `update_imported_docs` is triggered by each of the project.

The only line that could save this execution is 

https://github.com/rtfd/readthedocs.org/blob/445683fc0c86b0de56779447a8eedf98024dba32/readthedocs/core/views/hooks.py#L120

Related to #3520

Which file was modified to fix this bug?

Bug report: [shard-map] Check failed using unsafe_rbg_key
Based on code from @jheek :

```python
import os
os.environ['XLA_FLAGS'] = '--xla_force_host_platform_device_count=8'

from functools import partial

import numpy as np

import jax
import jax.numpy as jnp
from jax import random
from jax.experimental.shard_map import shard_map, PartitionSpec as P, Mesh

from flax import linen

jax.config.update('jax_enable_custom_prng', True)  # doesn't crash if we exclude this line!

dense = linen.Dense(128)

mesh = Mesh(np.array(jax.devices()).reshape(2, 4), ('model', 'data'))

@jax.jit
def f(rng):
  @partial(shard_map, mesh=mesh, in_specs=P("model"), out_specs=P("model"), check_rep=False)
  def g(rng):
    rng = rng[0]
    return dense.init(rng, jnp.zeros((8, 128)))

  return g(random.split(rng, 4))

f(random.PRNGKey(0)) # ==> crashes VM on ASSERT(IsManual(sharding))
```

Which file was modified to fix this bug?

Bug report: Don't create subscriptions for IgnoredOutputModules
This IgnoredOutputModules is a feature available only for TaskChain an Resubmission and it's mainly used by the recovery script (to recover ReReco lumis).

Needs more investigation, but it seems WorkQueueManager creates a subscription even for those output modules that we want to ignore, which later on brings problems in JobAccountant that will x-check the outputModules expected (database call) against the job outputs (which fails in this case).

Which file was modified to fix this bug?

Bug report: Inconsistent convention for complex derivatives
The convention for the handling of complex derivatives in JAX seems to be mixed. Consider the following two implementations of the abolute value function.
```py
import jax.numpy as np
from jax import grad

def f(x):
    return np.abs(x)

def g(x):
    return np.sqrt(np.real(x)**2 + np.imag(x)**2)

df = grad(f)
dg = grad(g)

print('gradient by df: ',df(1.0+0.5j))
print('gradient by dg: ',dg(1.0+0.5j))
```
The respective results differ by complex conjugation:
```
gradient by df:  (0.8944272-0.4472136j)
gradient by dg:  (0.8944272+0.4472136j)
```

Which file was modified to fix this bug?

Bug report: Cannot paste multiple statements into the console
### What was wrong?

Pasting multiple lines into the console at once results in a `SyntaxError`:

```python
>>> b = 123 
c = 338                                                                                                             
  File "<console>", line 1,
    b = 123
           ^
SyntaxError: multiple statements found while compiling a single statement
```

This issue didn't exist prior to adding `prompt_toolkit` so I think it must have something to do with `PromptSession`

### How can it be fixed?
Allow multiple statements to be pasted in at once. The behavior in `brownie console` in this regard should be the same as that of a regular python interpreter.

Which file was modified to fix this bug?

Bug report: jax.random.categorical doesn't respect shape parameter with broadcasted logits
### Description

The docs for `jax.random.categorical` say that the output of `jax.random.categorical` is `shape` when `shape` is provided. However, when provided `logits` which have to be broadcasted with `shape`, it just ignores the shape parameter after checking that it's broadcast compatible. I believe this can be fixed by broadcasting logits appropriately.

```
import jax
out = jax.random.categorical(jax.random.PRNGKey(0), logits=jax.numpy.ones( (4, 1, 10)), axis=-1, shape=(4, 8))

# out.shape is (4, 1), should be (4, 8)

assert out.shape == (4, 8), f"out.shape is {out.shape}, should be (4, 8)"
```



### What jax/jaxlib version are you using?

0.3.23

### Which accelerator(s) are you using?

CPU

### Additional system info

_No response_

### NVIDIA GPU info

_No response_

Which file was modified to fix this bug?

Bug report: Integer attribute value '1' displayed as 'True' in the client.
Motivation
----------
Rucio 1.20.2 and earlier, when the attribute value is set to an integer (e.g. for key='tier'), the value='1' is displayed as 'True' in the client output. Similarly, '0' is shown as 'False'.

According to FIXME comment in https://github.com/rucio/rucio/blob/5299d7b9b0e0c5240369916b7f05790a6f12b841/lib/rucio/db/sqla/types.py#L114-L120 this is a temporary work around legacy values in the ATLAS database, which should be removed after the database is fixed.

Bug report: DynamoDB2: querying incorrectly parses KeyConditionExpression
This is a bug for moto==1.3.14 .

When using an `AND` condition for DynamoDB moto fails to recognize the `AND` if it is not in all uppercase.  This is more restrictive than Dynamo DB which accepts lowercase `and` and returns the correct details.

AWS also has the lower case `and` usage in their docs. For example https://docs.aws.amazon.com/amazondynamodb/latest/developerguide/Query.html .  

This can be easily reproduced by creating a table and querying with a condition that matches the description as mentioned.

```
import boto3
from moto import mock_dynamodb2

table_name = 'Thread'
forum_name = 'ForumName'
subject = 'Subject'
table_definition = {
        "AttributeDefinitions": [
            {
                'AttributeName': forum_name,
                'AttributeType': 'S'
            },
            {
                'AttributeName': subject,
                'AttributeType': 'S'
            }
        ],
        "TableName": table_name,
        "KeySchema":...

Which file was modified to fix this bug?

Bug report: NoQaDirective line number affected by rendered Jinja2 trailing whitespace
Jinja2 trailing whitespace causes `NoQaDirective`s to be instantiated with different line numbers compared to where they are defined in a SQL file. This can cause `--noqa` comments to be ignored and errors to be incorrectly reported when running `sqlfluff lint`.

I tried to dig around the code, but I'm still not too familiar with the internals of `sqlfluff`. It appears to me that `--noqa` comments are being extracted from a templated version of the SQL file (as they respect Jinja2 removing trailing whitespace), where as the lining is running on a non-templated version of the SQL file (or at least a version that does not respect Jinja2 removing trailing whitespace).

## Minimal SQL
```
{%- set a_var = ["1", "2"] -%}

SELECT
  this_is_just_a_very_long_line_for_demostration_purposes AS wow_thats_big, --noqa: L016
  this_is_not_so_big
FROM
  a_table
```

## Expected Behaviour
Running `sqlfluff lint` should ignore line 4 being too long, and not report a Rule L016 violation, ...

Which file was modified to fix this bug?

Bug report: BatchNorm doesn't work with center or scale turned off
BatchNorm raises TypeError when using without center or scale or both. Seems pretty straightforward to fix (e.x. as a workaround I've set beta to None `beta = None if not center else beta[ed]` if center is False, the same for gamma but there might be some better solution).

Exception:
beta = beta[ed] # ed=(None, slice(None, None, None))
TypeError: tuple indices must be integers or slices, not tuple

Code:
```
from jax import np
from jax import random
from jax.experimental import stax


init_fn, apply_fn = stax.BatchNorm(axis=0, center=False, scale=False)
_, params = init_fn(random.PRNGKey(0), (-1, 1))

x = np.zeros((2, 1))
apply_fn(params, x)
```

Details:
jax==0.1.62
jaxlib==0.1.42

Which file was modified to fix this bug?

Bug report: NodePiece: The use of `complex_normalize` as a `relation_constrainer` results in relation embeddings that only have the value of 1 or -1.
### Describe the bug

When using the example NodePiece code suggested in the tutorial page, the use of `complex_normalize` results in relation embeddings that all either have the value 1 or -1. 

This seems to be because the `complex_normalize` function is actually expecting a complex tensor in order to work. I couldn't see a clear way to set the `dtype` arg for the relation representations within NodePiece.

Not sure if this is expected or if some changes need to be made to the NodePiece docs or with `complex_normalize` itself. 

### How to reproduce

Running the following:
```python 
from pykeen.models import NodePiece
from pykeen.pipeline import pipeline

result = pipeline(
    dataset="nations",
    dataset_kwargs=dict(
        create_inverse_triples=True,
    ),
    model=NodePiece,
    model_kwargs=dict(
        tokenizers=["RelationTokenizer"],
        num_tokens=[12],
        interaction="rotate",
        relation_constrainer="complex_normalize"
    ),
)
r...

Which file was modified to fix this bug?

Bug report: run/repro: check that --cwd is inside the repository
macOS High Sierra 10.13.6 (17G65)
```
$ python --version
Python 3.6.6
```

Reproduce MWE:

Clean installation:

```
$ virtualenv venv --python=python3.6
$ source venv/bin/activate
$ pip install dvc==0.19.13
$ git init
$ dvc init
```

Minimal example that works:

```
$ dvc run -o /tmp/input.txt `echo "Test" > /tmp/input.txt`
$ dvc run -d /tmp/input.txt -o /tmp/output.txt `wc /tmp/input.txt > /tmp/output.txt`
$ dvc repro output.txt.dvc
```

Minimal example that crashes:

```
$ dvc run --cwd /tmp -o /tmp/input.txt `echo "Test" > /tmp/input.txt`
$ dvc run --cwd /tmp -d /tmp/input.txt -o /tmp/output.txt `wc /tmp/input.txt > /tmp/output.txt`
$ dvc repro --cwd /tmp /tmp/output.txt.dvc
Error: Unexpected error: '../../../../tmp/output.txt.dvc'
```

Which file was modified to fix this bug?

Bug report: optimize_for_target_gateset OOMs on large CircuitOps
**Description of the issue**

When a circuit containing a large (in this case, 17 qubits & depth 20) CircuitOp is passed to `optimize_for_target_gateset`, it generates an OOM, possibly crashing the device. This circuit is well within the realm of "easy to simulate", so we shouldn't be OOMing or should at least warn users that this has a lower threshold than simulation.

**How to reproduce the issue**

```
qnum = 17
qubits = cirq.LineQubit.range(qnum)
subcircuit = cirq.FrozenCircuit(
    *[
      cirq.CX(qubits[i], qubits[i+1])
      for i in range(0, qnum-1, 2)
    ],
    *[
      cirq.CZ(qubits[i], qubits[i+1])
      for i in range(1, qnum-1, 2)
    ],
)
circuit = cirq.Circuit(
    cirq.CircuitOperation(subcircuit).repeat(10),
    cirq.measure(*qubits, key='out')
)
print(circuit)
# OOM: cannot allocate 256GB
supported_circuit = cirq.optimize_for_target_gateset(
    circuit,
    context=cirq.TransformerContext(deep=True),
    gateset=cirq.CZTargetGateset(),...

Which file was modified to fix this bug?

```python

Array([0, 0, 0, 0, ...], dtype=float32)

Bug report: `bring_online` parameter is submitted as a string, instead of int, to FTS
Motivation
----------
https://github.com/rucio/rucio/blob/e2ed93b6d315091cf4960f76b4253ce8fad27a6c/lib/rucio/daemons/conveyor/submitter.py#L107
Loads the value as a string. Later on in the workflow this is submitted to FTS as a string in the dictionary, which the new FTS3 REST interface on fts3-pilot does not like.

Modification
------------
This should be cast to int, or better loaded with `config_get_int`. Also applies to the stager.

Which file was modified to fix this bug?

Bug report: ReqMon DataCacheUpdate thread timing out
**Impact of the bug**
ReqMon (aka WMStats)

**Describe the bug**
Likely related to the very high load in the system, as explained here: https://github.com/dmwm/WMCore/issues/9668

Over the last days, WMStats became very sluggish and poking around the logs, I see many timeouts in the `DataCacheUpdate` reqmon cherrypy thread. It seems to have become much worse since yesterday.

From the logs, there are cases that it has to transfer ~200MB worth of data. Given that this is a general purpose, one can multiply that by 5 (1GB of data every 10 to 15min...). It also explains why wmstats sometimes loads and others it remains spinning forever.

**How to reproduce it**
Have 40k workflows in the system with many job failures.

**Expected behavior**
The obvious ones. Cache should always be up-to-date, thus avoiding these requests time out.
These caches were supposed to be "small" as well (<50MB), having such large caches continuously updated in many backends isn't health for the clus...

Which file was modified to fix this bug?

Bug report: SCLOPF returns TypeError with pyomo=False unless run with pyomo=True first
## Checklist

- [x] I am using the current [`master`](https://github.com/pypsa/pypsa/tree/master) branch or the latest [release](https://github.com/pypsa/pypsa/releases). Please indicate:

## Describe the Bug

Running `sclopf` with `pyomo=False` returns a concatenation-related `TypeError`. However, running `sclopf` with `pyomo=True` on the same network and then running with `pyomo=False` again works. carefully to prepare your net...

Bug report: [py2py3] Use pickle protocol=4 for job package in Python3 agents
**Impact of the bug**
WMAgent

**Describe the bug**
As we commission WMAgent in python3, it looks like we are not going to have a consistent python3 version between `rhel6` and `rhel7` OS, for:
* `rhel6`, thus slc6_*: we are planning to actually use the CMSSW python3.6.4
* `rhel7`, thus slc7_*: we are going to use our own COMP python3.8.2

Python 3.6.4 does not support pickle protocol=5, so we cannot simply create pickle files with the standard protocol in python 3.8.2. Otherwise it fails to be loaded during job runtime with:
```
Failed to load JobPackage:/srv/job/WMSandbox/JobPackage.pcl
unsupported pickle protocol: 5
```

**How to reproduce it**
Run a test workflow with Py3 WMAgent >= 1.5.1.pre6 (plus a few extra patches)

**Expected behavior**
My suggestion is to evaluate the python version when creating the job package in JobCreator
* if it's a python2, then simply create it with the highest protocol available
* if it's a python3 env, then create the pickle file...

Which file was modified to fix this bug?

Issue: [py2py3] Use pickle protocol=4 for job package in Python3 agents
**Impact of the bug**
WMAgent

**Describe the bug**
As we commission WMAgent in python3, it looks like we are not going to have a consistent python3 version between `rhel6` and `rhel7` OS, for:
* `rhel6`, thus slc6_*: we are planning to actually use the CMSSW python3.6.4
* `rhel7`, thus slc7_*: we are going to use our own COMP python3.8.2

Python 3.6.4 does not support pickle protocol=5, so we cannot simply create pickle files with the standard protocol in python 3.8.2. Otherwise it fails to be loaded during job runtime with:
```
Failed to load JobPackage:/srv/job/WMSandbox/JobPackage.pcl
unsupported pickle protocol: 5
```

**How to reproduce it**
Run a test workflow with Py3 WMAgent >= 1.5.1.pre6 (plus a few extra patches)

**Expected behavior**
My suggestion is to evaluate the python version when creating the job package in JobCreator
* if it's a python2, then simply create it with the highest protocol available
* if it's a python3 env, then create the pickle file...

Identify the file that contains the fix for this issue.

Bug report: Truncation of !roles Command Embed
Sentry Issue: [BOT-1Q](https://sentry.io/organizations/python-discord/issues/1531925673/?referrer=github_integration)

The `!roles` information command currently sticks role ID's & a corresponding mention into the `description` field of the embed:

https://github.com/python-discord/bot/blob/1ccc0f11fd4511d13c7cdf59070dca86465fe59f/bot/cogs/information.py#L31-L46

However, the `description` field has a character limit of `2048` characters and we now have more roles than can fit into this space so the command will raise an exception. We currently have `49` roles, the command generates around `45` characters per role, so math makes that `2205` characters.

Since this information is probably all useful to have, an alternative display mechanism will need to be utilized. Possible approaches include: pagination, filtering of roles to display, adjusting the information provided, etc.

Which file was modified to fix this bug?

Bug report: [1.7] regression in .dockerignore handling
If the `Dockerfile` is being ignored by a path in the `.dockerignore` file, it is incorrectly being removed from the context. There is a special case handling when the file is being excluded directly, but it should also apply when there is a path which includes the `Dockerfile`.

Possibly caused by #863

```
docker-py version: 1.7.0

$ tree -a
.
├── Docker
│   ├── dc.yml
│   └── Dockerfile.debug
└── .dockerignore

1 directory, 3 files

$ cat Docker/Dockerfile.debug

FROM alpine:3.3
RUN  echo building
CMD  echo run

$ cat Docker/dc.yml

app:
  build: ..
  dockerfile: Docker/Dockerfile.debug

$ cat .dockerignore
Docker/

docker-compose -f Docker/dc.yml build
ERROR: Cannot locate specified Dockerfile: Docker/Dockerfile.debug
```

Which file was modified to fix this bug?

Bug report: `cz ch <tag>` prints the whole changelog if `tag_format` is not set
### Description

Removing the `tag_format` config from the `pyproject.toml`  results in commitizen not being able to read the changelog for specific tags (it always prints the whole changelog). If `tag_format` is a required parameter it should be noted in the documentation. Otherwise `tag_format` should default to `$version`, since this is how it is initialized.

This can cause a lot of confusion for existing projects that are introducing commitizen in their workflows without using `cz init` and didn't add `tag_format` explicitly in the `pyproject.toml`. 

### Steps to reproduce

Give the following example setup

```bash
git init
cz init
git add . && git commit -m "feat: add pyproject"
cz bump
touch file1 && git add file1 && git commit -m "feat: add file1"
cz bump
```

Now check the changelog of the latest version

```bash
cz ch 0.2.0
cat CHANGELOG.md
## 0.2.0 (2022-11-27)

### Feat

- add file1
```

Remove `tag_format` line from `pyproject.toml`

Check again...

Which file was modified to fix this bug?

Issue: `cz ch <tag>` prints the whole changelog if `tag_format` is not set
### Description

Removing the `tag_format` config from the `pyproject.toml`  results in commitizen not being able to read the changelog for specific tags (it always prints the whole changelog). If `tag_format` is a required parameter it should be noted in the documentation. Otherwise `tag_format` should default to `$version`, since this is how it is initialized.

This can cause a lot of confusion for existing projects that are introducing commitizen in their workflows without using `cz init` and didn't add `tag_format` explicitly in the `pyproject.toml`. 

### Steps to reproduce

Give the following example setup

```bash
git init

Bug report: Set may be missing some available help channels after initialisation
Sentry issue: [BOT-Z1](https://sentry.io/organizations/python-discord/issues/2354932564/?referrer=github_integration)

If the cog is reloaded while there are less than the maximum amount of available channels, it makes some channels available until the limit is reached. When a channel is made available, it updates the `available_help_channels` set. The `update_available_help_channels()` function will not update this set if it sees that the set already contains elements. This results in only the channels that were just made available being in the set; the set will not contain the channels that were already available when the bot started.

```
KeyError: <TextChannel id=776184670794678303 name='help-mushroom' position=268 nsfw=False news=False category_id=696958401460043776>
(1 additional frame(s) were not displayed)
...
  File "bot/exts/help_channels/_cog.py", line 469, in on_message
    await self.claim_channel(message)
  File "bot/utils/lock.py", line 108, in wrapper
    log...

Which file was modified to fix this bug?

Bug report: sparse.eye calls Numpy preventing JIT
### Description

```
from jax import jit, numpy as jnp
from jax.experimental.sparse import eye


def f(A):
    n = A.shape[0]
    return eye(n)


A = jnp.ones([3, 3])
f(A)
# BCOO(float32[3, 3], nse=3)
jit(f)(A)
# TracerArrayConversionError ...

Bug report: Confusing `vmap` user experience when passing in `False` into `in_axes`
`False` is interpreted as 0 in the `in_axes` argument to `jax.vmap`. This is an invalid value (and some users may expect it to behave like `None`). See this code:

```python
import jax.numpy as jnp

def f(x, y):
  return jnp.sum(x) + jnp.sum(y)

print(jax.vmap(f, in_axes=[False, 0])(arr, arr))
print(jax.vmap(f, in_axes=[None, 0])(arr, arr))
print(jax.vmap(f, in_axes=[0, 0])(arr, arr))
```

prints

```
[ 6 14]
[13 17]
[ 6 14]
```

The `vmap` documentation states that each item in `in_axes` must be either an integer or None. @mbz was confused by this and accidentally thought that `False` would behave like `None`. But instead it's interpreted as 0.

It would be good to raise an error for users accidentally using `False` in `in_axes`.

Which file was modified to fix this bug?

Bug report: Cancel scheduled tasks when unloading cogs if those tasks will get rescheduled on reload
Sentry Issue: [BOT-7H](https://sentry.io/organizations/python-discord/issues/1813694114/?referrer=github_integration)

This started off as an issue to ignore 404s for deleting offensive messages from our database, but after thinking about it some more, these 404s never should have happened. It turns out there were a duplicate tasks scheduled to delete the same messages. This happened because the filtering cog was reloaded. The cog re-schedules the tasks when it's initialised, but it does so with a new `Scheduler` instance, which means it cannot tell that it's trying to schedule duplicates.

Which file was modified to fix this bug?

Bug report: [Bug] Pulling image is now based on `push_time` which is not updated if image is identical
**Describe the bug**
In `pull_if_newer()`, we pull the image if the remote timestamp is newer than the local one. Recently, we changed this (https://github.com/vantage6/vantage6/pull/482), and based the timestamp on the push time. 

It can occur that the local and remote image are identical, but that the remote push timestamp is newer. Then, the 'newer' remote image is pulled over and over again without ever updating the timestamp. This should be achieved somehow so that no pulls are attempted.

Note that when the images are identical, the pull time is short, so this issue is not very pressing for that reason.

Which file was modified to fix this bug?

Bug report: aiohttp.AsyncResolver needs to be created inside an async function
Despite the efforts in #681, I failed to realise `aiohttp.AsyncResolver` suffers from the same `DeprecationWarning` as the session. 

Thankfully, it seems that the resolver of a `aiohttp.TCPConnector` instance can be swapped out whenever. It just uses whatever the value of `self._resolver` is every time it needs to resolve a host. It is a "private" attribute but ostensibly there isn't any reason it shouldn't be changed. 

So, the resolver can simply be created in `Bot.start()`. I believe it is safe to also close the resolver and even the connector in `Bot.close()`. I was mainly concerned by how discord.py would handle this. The connector goes to discord.py's `HTTPClient` which uses it to create an internal `aiohttp.ClientSession` - basically the same thing we use the connector for. Whenever `Client.clear()` is called, the session gets recreated using `HTTPClient._connector`. Therefore, the connector can be re-created and assigned to that attribute before `Client.clear()` is called....

Which file was modified to fix this bug?

Issue: aiohttp.AsyncResolver needs to be created inside an async function
Despite the efforts in #681, I failed to realise `aiohttp.AsyncResolver` suffers from the same `DeprecationWarning` as the session. 

Thankfully, it seems that the resolver of a `aiohttp.TCPConnector` instance can be swapped out whenever. It just uses whatever the value of `self._resolver` is every time it needs to resolve a host. It is a "private" attribute but ostensibly there isn't any reason it shouldn't be changed. 

So, the resolver can simply be created in `Bot.start()`. I believe it is safe to also close the resolver and even the connector in `Bot.close()`. I was mainly concerned by how discord.py would handle this. The connector goes to discord.py's `HTTPClient` which uses it to create an internal `aiohttp.ClientSession` - basically the same thing we use the connector for. Whenever `Client.clear()` is called, the session gets recreated using `HTTPClient._connector`. Therefore, the connector can be re-created and assigned to that attribute before `Client.clear()` is called....

Identify the file that contains the fix for this issue.

Bug report: `ModelBuilder` doesn't support `LazyTableReference`
If we have a table like this:

```python
class Band(Table):
    manager = ForeignKey(LazyTableReference('Manager', module_path='some.other.folder.tables'))
```

It breaks `ModelBuilder`.

`LazyTableReference` is used when we have circular references between tables because of `ForeignKey`s.

Which file was modified to fix this bug?

Bug report: Problems when using parallel processing with threads and multiple languages
There's a problem with Nikola if your site has more than one language and if you use `nikola run -n4 -P thread` (run four tasks in parallel using threads instead of forking, see here: http://pydoit.org/cmd_run.html#parallel-execution). Namely, when rendering a page/..., `LocaleBorg` is storing the current language in global storage which is then used by `messages("...")` and `formatted_date()` (among others). When two tasks run in parallel for two different languages, one will overwrite the `LocaleBorg` setup of the other, and with high probability one of the output files ends up with wrong strings or wrongly formatted dates.
(When using forking, this should not be a problem, but that doesn't always work -- it might be problematic under Windows, and totally breaks for the `earlytask_impl` branch.)

I don't know how easy/complicated fixing this would be. There are two aspects:
1. If we store the current language not in a global state, but a thread-local state, at least `messages("...")`...

Which file was modified to fix this bug?

Bug report: Reference User Identicon
Currently, `reference.detail.users` array does not return the user's identicon field, whereas the `users.list.documents` array entries do. 
When rendering the reference's assigned user listings, the identicon field is copied over from its corresponding `users.list.documents` entry.
Assigning a new user to a reference results in a PATCH request that will add the identicon field to the new `reference.detail.users` array entry.

This should be done on the server!

Bug report: named_parameter_priors won't get priors for children of torch Modules
I think the PR #196  I did fixed things like DKL to at least run, but was a bad fix because we'll fail to get priors for parameters in gpytorch.Modules that are children of torch.nn.Modules. For example, consider AdditiveKernel, which places a bunch of Kernels in a ModuleList. Since named_parameter_priors is a recursive call that won't actually run on the ModuleList because it doesn't exist, we won't actually get priors for the component Kernels even if they are defined.

Ordinarily I'd just implement a fix, but I'm actually not sure how to deal with this one. Even if we implement an in house ModuleList extension, it seems dangerous and insidious to leave this failure case in, since the intention is that GPyTorch Modules are interoperable with torch ones. Therefore, a recursive version of named_parameter_priors seems not possible -- maybe we have to expand each child of a normal torch Module in the method directly?

@Balandat

Which file was modified to fix this bug?

Bug report: [beta] RubyLinter is not lazy
As mentioned in #782 `RubyLinter` loads eager. It is thus prone to err on fast start-ups on OSX bc if the `env[PATH]` is not immediately ready and good to use, it will disable itself bc it cannot find any reasonable executable.

Note: `RubyLinter` has its own interpretation of how and what extension points `Linter` provides. It should really implement `can_lint` and `context_sensitive_executable_path` to be lazy.

Which file was modified to fix this bug?

Bug report: Cannot create Copr project when identifier contains not allowed characters
Sentry Issue: [PACKIT-SERVICE-4N2](https://sentry.io/organizations/red-hat-0p/issues/3545446876/?referrer=github_integration)

```
Cannot create a new Copr project (owner=packit-stg project=packit-specfile-91-fedora+epel chroots=['fedora-rawhide-x86_64', 'epel-9-x86_64', 'fedora-36-x86_64', 'fedora-35-x86_64']): name: Name must contain only letters, digits, underscores, dashes and dots.
```

@lbarcziova: we pass the job identifier directly as a suffix to the Copr project name, so in case the identifier contains not allowed characters, the Copr project cannot be created. The reporting in this case is also [confusing](https://github.com/packit/specfile/pull/91#issuecomment-1231939314). When creating the default Copr project name, for commit trigger we include the branch name, so that may cause the same issues.

TODO:
- [ ] check the Copr project name to be created and remove/modify the unwanted characters
- [ ] improve the reporting

Which file was modified to fix this bug?

Issue: Cannot create Copr project when identifier contains not allowed characters

```
Cannot create a new Copr project (owner=packit-stg project=packit-specfile-91-fedora+epel chroots=['fedora-rawhide-x86_64', 'epel-9-x86_64', 'fedora-36-x86_64', 'fedora-35-x86_64']): name: Name must contain only letters, digits, underscores, dashes and dots.
```

@lbarcziova: we pass the job identifier directly as a suffix to the Copr project name, so in case the identifier contains not allowed characters, the Copr project cannot be created. The reporting in this case is also [confusing](https://github.com/packit/specfile/pull/91#issuecomment-1231939314). When creating the default Copr project name, for commit trigger we include the branch name, so that may cause the same issues.

Bug report: VartypeView.relabel_variables can return model with wrong vartype
**Description**
`VartypeView.relabel_variable` acts on base model and returns a wrong (base) vartype if `base.vartype != view.vartype`.

**Steps To Reproduce**
```
In [1]: import dimod

In [2]: view = dimod.BQM.from_ising({0: 1}, {}).binary

In [3]: rel = view.relabel_variables({0: 0}, inplace=False)

In [4]: rel == view
Out[4]: False

In [5]: view
Out[5]: BinaryView({0: 2}, {}, -1, 'BINARY')

In [6]: rel
Out[6]: BinaryQuadraticModel({0: 1}, {}, 0, 'SPIN')
```

**Expected Behavior**
```
In [1]: import dimod

In [2]: view = dimod.BQM.from_ising({0: 1}, {}).binary

In [3]: rel = view.relabel_variables({0: 0}, inplace=False)

In [4]: rel == view
Out[4]: True

In [5]: view
Out[5]: BinaryView({0: 2}, {}, -1, 'BINARY')

In [6]: rel
Out[6]: BinaryView({0: 2}, {}, -1, 'BINARY')
```

**Additional Context**
Causes https://github.com/dwavesystems/dwave-system/issues/333.

Which file was modified to fix this bug?

Bug report: Dark reaper crashes due to None scope
Motivation
----------
The dark-reaper crashes with

```
2019-08-29 13:56:57,631 18591   CRITICAL        Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/usr/lib/python2.7/site-packages/rucio/daemons/reaper/dark_reaper.py", line 106, in reaper
    lfns=[{'scope': replica['scope'].external, 'name': replica['name'], 'path': replica['path']}],
AttributeError: 'NoneType' object has no attribute 'external'
```

This is due to the fact that for quarantined replicas the scope (and name) can sometimes be NULL. But due to the InternalScope change where the external is forcefully read, the daemon crashes. This needs to be adapted to handle NULL scopes.

Which file was modified to fix this bug?

Issue: Dark reaper crashes due to None scope
Motivation
----------
The dark-reaper crashes with

```
2019-08-29 13:56:57,631 18591   CRITICAL        Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/usr/lib/python2.7/site-packages/rucio/daemons/reaper/dark_reaper.py", line 106, in reaper
    lfns=[{'scope': replica['scope'].external, 'name': replica['name'], 'path': replica['path']}],
AttributeError: 'NoneType' object has no attribute 'external'
```

This is due to the fact that for quarantined replicas the scope (and name) can sometimes be NULL. But due to the InternalScope change where the external is forcefully read, the daemon crashes. This needs to be adapted to handle NULL scopes.

Bug report: Submissions emails should only be sent for new submissions
While #656 fixed the number of emails we're getting, the problem of getting approval emails for every single change of a record still isn't fixed.

We should really only be getting emails with the subject "Job Submitted for Approval" for new submissions.

It may make sense to also get emails for changes to existing records, but those should then be using a different subject, e.g. "Job Edited", but I'm not sure whether that's really needed.

Bug report: ManagedApplication does not garbage collect
**Overview:**
When a `ManagedApplication` is created and then dereferenced from that context, it is not garbage collected.

**Development notes:**
I'll be pushing a test for this shortly.

Even with a cleansing pass and a thorough `close()` method, something is hanging on to this object.  I suspect it is the `ProcController` or something in the appctl chain.  We'll want to add tests for these classes too.

The gist of testing for garbage collection:

```
from lg_common import ManagedApplication
import gc
import weakref

app = ManagedApplication(cmd=['/bin/cat'])
app_ref = weakref.ref(app)
app.close()
app = None
gc.collect()
assert app_ref() is None
```

Which file was modified to fix this bug?

Bug report: Use --all-branches and --all-tags at the same time

It seems that it is not possible currently to use `--all-branches` and `--all-tags` at the same time, as we can see [there](https://github.com/iterative/dvc/blob/master/dvc/scm.py#L66): because of the `elif`, only one option can be used.

Am I right?

Bug report: Boolean indexing/masking with the wrong shape should fail
This example results in an error with NumPy but not jax.numpy:
```python
import jax.numpy as jnp

x = jnp.arange(4)
y = jnp.array([True, False])
x[y]   # DeviceArray([0], dtype=int32)
```

It looks like JAX converts the boolean indexer array into an integer array for indexing without checking the shape.

We should raise an error in this case: indexing an array of shape (4,) with a boolean array of shape (2,) is never valid.

Which file was modified to fix this bug?

Issue: Boolean indexing/masking with the wrong shape should fail
This example results in an error with NumPy but not jax.numpy:
```python
import jax.numpy as jnp

x = jnp.arange(4)

It looks like JAX converts the boolean indexer array into an integer array for indexing without checking the shape.

We should raise an error in this case: indexing an array of shape (4,) with a boolean array of shape (2,) is never valid.

Bug report: Sparse argmin/argmax return scalar values on CPU
CuPy returns zero-dimensional ndarrays for reduction functions where NumPy/SciPy return scalar values to keep off synchronizing between GPU and CPU. However, CuPy's sparse matrix argmin/argmax currently return scalar values as SciPy's counterparts, causing device to host sync.

```Python
>>> import cupy
>>> import cupyx
>>> a = cupy.array([[0,1,0],[2,0,3]], dtype='f')
>>> cupyx.scipy.sparse.csc_matrix(a).argmin()
0
```

Which file was modified to fix this bug?

Bug report: Status bar busy indicator stops on first response from backend
We only fire 'BEGIN_LINTING' and 'FINISHED_LINTING'. But since #887 the responses fly in as soon as possible. Currently all responses fire 'FINISHED_LINTING'. 

We need three events here

'START_LINTING(bid)' -> 'LINT_RESULT(bid, linter, errors)' -> 'STOP_LINTING(bid [,errors])'

Feel free to bike-shed the naming here.

Which file was modified to fix this bug?

Bug report: Checkin causes sporadic IntegrityError exceptions
**Describe the bug**
When clients checkin, Sal processes their ManagedInstalls, and the results of any current installs or removals. Because some of this data does not include dates, Sal provides a date based on the checkin time. This unfortunately creates `UpdateHistoryItem` objects that are out-of-sequence when `sort_by('recorded')` is used later to determine whether to create new items. The checkin script then plows ahead with creating an item that will then fail the uniqueness requirements of the `UpdateHistoryItem` model.

I'm not 100% I can create this situation manually in anything but a synthetic way, but I see it in my fleet.

3. On the next checkin, for some reason the ManagedInstallReport still has an install result entry. (I guess this is because Sal is checking i...

Bug report: checkout: don't remove other links when checking out particular dvc file
Currently `dvc checkout`(and `dvc pull`) improperly collect all stages to remove unused links. When a target is specified, links are collected only for target stages https://github.com/iterative/dvc/blob/0.32.1/dvc/repo/checkout.py#L27 and then all other links are removed https://github.com/iterative/dvc/blob/0.32.1/dvc/repo/checkout.py#L31 , which is not correct.

Which file was modified to fix this bug?

Bug report: logger: handle chained exceptions
Current logger logic introduced in https://github.com/iterative/dvc/pull/1454 doesn't handle chained exceptions as the old logic did. Since we need to support both python3 and python2, which doesn't have built-in support for exception chaining, we use our own chaining mechanism https://github.com/iterative/dvc/blob/master/dvc/exceptions.py#L6 . See https://github.com/iterative/dvc/blob/0.23.2/dvc/logger.py#L100 .

Which file was modified to fix this bug?

Bug report: Notification configuration is not cascading properly
**Describe the bug**
The introduction of tags in v0.43 has broken notification configurations from cascading properly. Global settings are being used entirely (instead of granularly) whenever the notification URL is not present in the watch nor the tag config.

**Version**
0.43

**To Reproduce**

Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Add global notification settings to include a URL
2. Create a new watch with: no tags, no notification URL's, but with a title and body

**Expected behavior**
The watch should use title and body from the individual watch settings, but inherit the URL (and anything else that wasn't individually specified) from the global settings.

Instead, only global settings are used, skipping the title and body settings from the watch.

**Additional context**
Changes in `update_worker.py` lines 91-140 (52f2c00) are problematic and should be refactored to not have separate `if not sent:` branches for each layer (watch, tag, global). Since each setting sho...

Which file was modified to fix this bug?

Bug report: Updating a cluster causes downtime for worker nodes
Updating the cluster with `./cluster.py update <stack-name> <version>` will currently lead to a downtime for worker nodes as we are not waiting for node readiness (we just wait for ASG `InService` which does not indicate whether the node is completely up and registered).

NOTE: there is no downtime for master nodes while updating as we already correctly wait/check the ELB status for the master API server.

Which file was modified to fix this bug?

Bug report: sdk does not support data api skysatvideo item type
**Expected behavior**
`planet data search skysatvideo` should search for the [skysatvideo](https://developers.planet.com/docs/data/skysatvideo/) item type.

**Actual behavior (describe the problem)**
skysatvideo is not supported

```console
planet-client-python$> planet data search skysatvideo
Usage: planet data search [OPTIONS] ITEM_TYPES
Try 'planet data search --help' for help.

Error: Invalid value for 'ITEM_TYPES': item_type - 'skysatvideo' is not one of 'PSOrthoTile', 'PSScene', 'MYD09GQ', 'Sentinel2L1C', 'SkySatCollect', 'REOrthoTile', 'REScene', 'MOD09GA', 'Sentinel1', 'Landsat8L1G', 'MYD09GA', 'MOD09GQ', 'SkySatScene'.

skysatvideo is a valid item type for the data api. the sdk pulls valid item types from the orders api bundle spec, which does not include skysatvideo. skysatvideo is not mentioned in the data api [openapi spec](https://api.planet.com/data/v1/spec). it is described in the [docs](https://developers.planet.com/docs/data/skysatvideo/)...

Bug report: requests stay forever in Queued state if source creation fails
Motivation
----------
If an exception occurs while trying to create sources on submission, the database transaction is rollbacked and the requests remain
in queued state. 


Modification
------------

The requests should be transited to SUBMISSION_FAILED state.

Which file was modified to fix this bug?

Issue: requests stay forever in Queued state if source creation fails
Motivation
----------
If an exception occurs while trying to create sources on submission, the database transaction is rollbacked and the requests remain
in queued state. 


Modification
------------

The requests should be transited to SUBMISSION_FAILED state.

Bug report: Make sure workload_fio_storageutilization fixtures waits for the storage to be actually reclaimed
When test case(s) in a run use multiple different `workload_storageutilization_*` fixtures, it could happen that while OCS is still deleting data written by one just finished fixture, another storage utilization fixture is already started, thinking that there is a lot of data, but by the time it starts writing,  the space is finaly reclaimed so that the actual global utilization (based on a measurement when the utilization was still high because of the previous fixture) is nowhere near the expected target of the 2nd fixture.

Which file was modified to fix this bug?

Bug report: Improve getting of the Testing Farm composes
When getting a TF compose, we sanitize the target in [distro2compose method](https://github.com/packit/packit-service/blob/dad5a6d1c5eea1feb15a95652804cfc84083c0ca/packit_service/worker/helpers/testing_farm.py#L336). This breaks some usecases, e.g. having `RHEL-7.8-ZStream`  in the configuration results in `RHEL-7.8-Zstream` compose  (because of `title()`)  that is not in the list of available composes and therefore the test run fails ([example](https://github.com/r0x0d/leapp-packit-poc/pull/3/checks?check_run_id=8308160405)).

We could firstly check the raw distro (the target without arch) and if it is in the list of available composes, don't sanitize it. Or we could try to improve the sanitization itself, but we need to make sure we don't break the other usecases.

Which file was modified to fix this bug?

Bug report: jnp.mean and jnp.std crash on large arrays.
### Description


I have a large array of shape float32[16,32,1280,4096].  When I call jnp.std on this array, jit crashes and fails with the following error:  "Python int 2684354560 too large to convert to int32".

Clearly the implementation of jnp.std is dividing by the number of elements in the array, which in this case does not fit into a 32-bit integer.

v0.4.2

TPU

Check your key and signing method.sequence of bytes

Bug report: Wrong query and problem due to overlapping partition
Motivation
----------
Two problems were noticed :
- Problem with the protocols query in `get_pfn_to_rse` :
```
/usr/local/lib/python3.6/site-packages/rucio/core/replica.py:516: SAWarning: SELECT statement has a cartesian product between FROM element(s) "atlas_rucio.rse_protocols" and FROM element "atlas_rucio.rses".  Apply join condition(s) between each element to resolve.
  for rse_id, protocol, hostname, port, prefix in query.yield_per(10000):
  ```
- Problem due to heartbeat that expires before the end of a cycle resulting in overlapping partitions. Moreover when the partititions overlap, the `ORA-00054` raised is not handled properly and it is considered as a `CRITICAL` error.

Bug report: VO missing from reaper messages
Motivation
----------

Hermes messages for transfers etc. include the VO as part of the payload when it is not `def` (i.e. in M-VO mode only). This allows messages to be sorted by VO when monitoring. Currently, none of the reapers include the VO as part of the message payload.

Modification
------------

Use the scope of the deleted replica to get the VO, and include it in the payload for all reaper daemons.

Which file was modified to fix this bug?

Bug report: `transfer-queued` message does not include the correct services
Motivation
----------
With all messages handled by the new hermes2 logic, messages get attached to the services defined in the configuration. This is then always handled when calling `add_message`. However, the `queue_requests` workflow does not call `add_message` but instead bulk inserts the messages to the database, not respecting the services logic.

https://github.com/rucio/rucio/blob/a5687ef8469e62e6466c8c26deaf306b3c0d7c9a/lib/rucio/core/request.py#L323-L324

Modification
------------
Either `add_message` needs to be called here, or better create a bulk logic for `add_message` which can be called here to have the advantage of the bulk inserts.

Which file was modified to fix this bug?

Bug report: Nonintuitive indices on positions of head_related_impulse_responses
## General

* pyfar version: 0.5.1
* Python version: 3.10
* Operating System: Windows 11
* Did you install pyfar via pip: yes

## Description

When using `pf.signals.files.head_related_impulse_responses` to get HRIRs for multiple positions, I expected the indices to align with the order of items in the passed list. 

E.g. `[270,0],[0,0],[90,0]` => same order indices `0,1,2`.

Instead:
- index 0 => position [0,0]
- index 1 => position [90,0]
- index 2 => position [270,0]

## What I Did

```
import pyfar as pf
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
hrir, _ = pf.signals.files.head_related_impulse_responses([[270,0],[0,0],[90,0]],diffuse_field_compensation=True)
pf.plot.time_freq(hrir[0])
pf.plot.time_freq(hrir[1])
pf.plot.time_freq(hrir[2])
plt.show()
# -> looking at the plots reveals the "non-matching" order

# alternatively:
import sounddevice
out=pf.dsp.convolve(hrir[0],pf.signals.files.speech())
sounddevice.play(out.time.T,out.sampling_rate,blocking=True)
# ...

Which file was modified to fix this bug?

Bug report: LumiList not working properly for TaskChain
If one provides the LumiList in the main dict of a TaskChain request, it will be taken into consideration for the job splitting and one can see the "splitting.lumis" parameter in the workload.
However, this is not how TaskChain works, these kind of parameters should always go inside a specific Task(s), which does not work in the current code.

One can use this request
https://github.com/dmwm/WMCore/blob/master/test/data/ReqMgr/requests/DMWM/TaskChain_Data.json
and add this line to the Task1 dict

```
       "LumiList": {"160960": [[277, 287], [79,85], [167,177], [226,232]]},
```

in order to reproduce this failure.

Which file was modified to fix this bug?

Bug report: memoryMap bound to class creates concurrency issues
There is an issue where, when you have created multiple boards of the same type and try to flash/erase them in parallel you get concurrency issues.

This is in some part due to the fact that the MemoryMap for the targets are bound to the class rather than the instance. This means that associated FlashRegions are not unique per board instance and these have some mutating state in which you get pretty reliable race-conditions.

This was verified by overloading the builtin targets and moving MemoryMap() to __init__. It would be better to have this problem fixed in pyocd though.

Which file was modified to fix this bug?

Bug report: Derivation of co2s is not correct
Right now, the calculation of `co2s` (CO2 mole fraction at surface level) is scientifically wrong. Instead of calculating the mole fraction at the surface level, it calculates the mole fraction at the lowest unmasked level. For the correct calculation, it is necessary to interpolate `co2` on the surface air pressure (`ps`). Thanks @bettina-gier for raising this and @axel-lauer for the advice!

Which file was modified to fix this bug?

Bug report: [convert] file series fail because of None in attrs
When merging a file series with *silx convert*, the process now fails because the "file" attribute *@filename* is *None*. 
This causes the following error:
`TypeError: Object dtype dtype('O') has no native HDF5 equivalent`

I think that previously it didn't crash because the was a coercion of all attributes to a dtype *numpy.string_*, so we ended up with a *@filename=b"None"*. 

I'm not sure if this is a `fabioh5` bug or a `silx.io.convert` bug.

In `silx.io.convert`, this patch fixes the issue:

```
diff --git a/silx/io/convert.py b/silx/io/convert.py
index 3af2c44..a2839fd 100644
--- a/silx/io/convert.py
+++ b/silx/io/convert.py
@@ -165,8 +165,9 @@ class Hdf5Writer(object):
         root_grp = h5f[self.h5path]
         for key in infile.attrs:
             if self.overwrite_data or key not in root_grp.attrs:
-                root_grp.attrs.create(key,
-                                      infile.attrs[key])
+                if infile.attrs[key] is not None:
+  ...

Which file was modified to fix this bug?

Bug report: 0.63.1 breaks D builds with LDC when external linker flags are set
Hi!
At the moment all D packages that use Meson (which is quite a bunch!) fail to build in Debian with the upgrade to Meson 0.63.1 (0.63.0 was fine). Debian sets the `LDFLAGS` env var to set specific linker flags, which Meson passes through to the *compiler* in verbatim. The LDC D compiler can't understand GNU linker flags on its own though, they need to be preprocessed as done in the D compiler backend of Meson: https://github.com/mesonbuild/meson/blob/master/mesonbuild/compilers/d.py#L286

The commit that highly likely broke this is https://github.com/mesonbuild/meson/commit/0ec039d2597526c670f7e0478a7b5008e14ff474, see https://bugs.debian.org/cgi-bin/bugreport.cgi?bug=1017087 for the Debian bugreport.

**To Reproduce**
Just set `LDFLAGS=-Wl,-z,relro` and try to build the D examples from the testsuite.

**Expected behavior**
To determine the linker, Meson should either not pass through all linker flags from the environment, or (probably better), if they are sent to the compi...

Which file was modified to fix this bug?

Bug report: policy_filter decorator makes methods fail in certain cases
Motivation
----------
When calling `lifetime = get_scratch_policy(account, rses, lifetime, session=session)` the `policy_filter` is applied to the method. However, if an organisation (such as generic) is set which is not in the mapping, the method is replaced by a null_funct, returning a NoneType. Thus in cases where the line above is called (with a lifetime of eg 100) it is replaced by `None`.

```
$ rucio add-rule user.mlassnig:my-test-dataset --lifetime 100 2 "MANCHESTER|CAMBRIDGE"
f69edfedf5de494397550640877a0974
$ rucio rule-info f69edfedf5de494397550640877a0974
...
Expires at:                 None
...
```

When doing update_rule with lifetime, the method even crashes completely, as it cannot handle the Nonetype in that case.

Expected behaviour
--------------

In cases where no respective method is set, the lifetime argument should just be passed through.

Which file was modified to fix this bug?

Bug report: dvcignore: not using tree
https://github.com/iterative/dvc/blob/0.66.6/dvc/ignore.py#L67
It is using os.walk, which means that no matter which branch we are checked into in the repo.tree, we are still using dvcignores from the current workspace. Need to make it use `repo.tree`. Probably by passing `self` instead of `self.root_dir` here https://github.com/iterative/dvc/blob/0.66.6/dvc/repo/__init__.py#L499 .

Discovered during the investigation for https://github.com/iterative/dvc/issues/2737

Bug report: Rename column causes persistent rename column prompt in migration manager
If you rename a column in a Table and auto generate a migration you will be asked if you renamed a column. Answering "Y" and running the migration gives you the correct behavior. However, on subsequent migrations you are asked about the exact same column again. Also note that when renaming multiple columns answering "N" to the first prompt stops further ones. Additionally, The name pairings in the prompt aren't matched up properly.

This can be demonstrated using the basic asgi scaffold.

To get started...
```console
> piccolo asgi new
> piccolo migrations new home --auto
> piccolo migrations forwards home
```

- Then go to `./home/tables.py` and change the `completed` columns to `success` or anything other name.
- Another migration which will run fine
```console
> piccolo migrations new home --auto
Creating new migration ...
Did you rename the `completed` column to `success` on the `Task` table? (y/N)
> y
> piccolo migrations forwards home
```
Now go update the tab...

Which file was modified to fix this bug?
